signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.081% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.872% (n=7), creating a 1.953% spread; the 290bp US-JGB yield gap runs against JPY momentum, setting up a potential squeeze risk due to crowded short -88.5k positions. The next BoJ meeting on 28 Apr may resolve this setup, especially considering the yield disadvantage and the strong G8 theme.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA at 4.10% with a hike bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The +35bp carry advantage from RBA 4.10% vs Fed 3.75% supports the pair. The next RBA meeting on 5 May determines direction in resolving this setup.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Tariff Escalation
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The ECB rate at 2.00% and the Fed rate at 3.75% create a -175bp carry disadvantage for EUR. The next ECB meeting is likely to influence the pair's direction.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; BoE at 3.75% with a Hold bias (next meeting) is likely to sustain the pair, considering the +133bp carry advantage over the BoJ (BoE 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). However, the GBP COT long +32.6k (67.3% long) indicates potential long unwind risk if the BoE surprises with a hike.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Impact
signals.text: USD/CAD closed Friday at 1.3588; WTI crude oil price movements is likely to influence the pair, especially considering the -150bp carry disadvantage from BoC 2.25% vs Fed 3.75%. The next BoC meeting will be important for resolving the pair's direction.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: With NZD/USD at 0.5898, the -150bp carry differential favors the USD, but specs are short −16.8k, positioning against the RBNZ's hike bias, where 40% of a hike is priced. This sets up a short-squeeze risk as the market is positioned against the direction the RBNZ is signalling, creating an asymmetric squeeze risk. The next RBNZ meeting on 27 May is likely to be the catalyst for this unwind.
